items.title: Amasty issues massive security release for 25 extensions, one critical RCE
items.summary: Amasty released a security update for 25 extensions, including one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: A critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer allows remote code execution via a crafted CacheWarmer cookie. CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including critical issues that could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Adobe Commerce DoS Vulnerability (Uncontrolled Resource Consumption)
items.summary: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit this denial-of-service flaw in Adobe Commerce by sending crafted network requests that exhaust system resources, disrupting availability for legitimate users.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Auth Bypass Vulnerability (Incorrect Authorization)
items.summary: This incorrect authorization flaw in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the application.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce via REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw allowing unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to Magento/Adobe Commerce stores via the REST API, with attacks observed on many stores. Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9, but it was not backported to older supported versions.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.

items.title: Adobe Commerce SSRF Vulnerability
items.summary: This Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Adobe Commerce allows high-privileged attackers to bypass security features and access unauthorized internal or external resources.
